name: Pink Cascade
shortDesc: Soft blush petals with a deeper rose lip. Multiple spikes, long bloom season of 3–5 months. 5" pot.
longDesc: Soft blush petals with a deeper rose lip and a subtle white centre. One of our most consistently reliable performers — multiple spikes mean blooms that overlap and extend the season well beyond a single-spike variety. An ideal first orchid or a dependable repeat gift.

name: Ivory Grace
shortDesc: Classic pure white blooms with a yellow-gold center. A timeless choice for weddings and gifting. 4" pot.
longDesc: Classic pure white blooms with a warm yellow-gold centre. Derived from the wild Phalaenopsis amabilis species native to Southeast Asia — a timeless choice for weddings, gifting, and understated interior arrangements. The clean white holds exceptionally well under warm household lighting.

name: Midnight Haze
shortDesc: Deep lavender petals fading to violet. Striking specimen plant with up to 18 flowers per spike. 6" pot.
longDesc: Deep lavender petals that graduate to violet at the edges. A striking specimen plant grown to a larger scale — up to 18 flowers per spike when conditions are right. Grown in a 6" pot with room to develop a second spike, this is a statement plant for collectors and enthusiasts.

name: Golden Hour
shortDesc: Warm yellow blooms with a subtle orange blush at the center. Cheerful and long-lasting. 5" pot.
longDesc: Warm yellow blooms with a subtle orange blush radiating from the centre lip. Cheerful and long-lasting, this variety holds its colour well under household lighting — an unusual find in warm tones among phalaenopsis. Pairs beautifully with neutral interiors.

name: Sunrise Stripe
shortDesc: Boldly striped petals in blush and cream. Each bloom is uniquely patterned — no two are identical. 5" pot.
longDesc: Boldly striped petals in alternating blush and cream. Because the stripe pattern emerges from natural pigmentation variation, each bloom is uniquely patterned — no two plants from the same batch are ever identical. A conversation piece as much as a plant.

name: Speckled Beauty
shortDesc: Creamy white petals dotted with lavender speckles. A rare species-type beloved by collectors. 4" pot.
longDesc: Creamy white petals covered in fine lavender speckles arranged in irregular hieroglyphic-like markings — the trait that gives this species its scientific name. A rare species-type (not a hybrid) beloved by serious collectors and orchid societies. Stock is very limited each season.
